items.title: Severn Trent avoids fine for wastewater failures
items.summary: The water firm took "genuine accountability" after breaching wastewater obligations, Ofwat says.

items.title: Oil jumps over 6% as Trump declares Iran ceasefire, MoU effectively over
items.summary: The US has reinstated sanctions on Iranian oil and launches fresh strikes, reviving fears of disruption in the Strait of Hormuz and raising fresh concerns for India's energy security and inflation outlook.

items.title: Indian-flagged crude tanker turns back from Strait of Hormuz after vessel attacks: Report
items.summary: Several oil and gas tankers have turned back from the Strait of Hormuz. This action follows renewed attacks on vessels in the critical waterway. A Qatari LNG tanker and a Saudi crude oil tanker were damaged near the strait. Maritime authorities have raised the threat risk for transiting vessels to severe. Indian refiner Mangalore Refinery cancelled a crude oil charter from Iraq.

items.title: Broker’s Call: Minda Corp (Buy)
items.summary: Monarch Networth Capital

items.title: India seeks safe Hormuz passage for nine ships as truce falters
items.summary: In a strategic move, India is engaging Iran in talks to guarantee the safety of nine tankers loaded with crude oil and liquefied petroleum gas. With vigilant oversight from the government on these vessels, officials will confer with Iranian counterparts to discuss the precarious state of maritime security due to regional tensions that threaten both shipments and a delicate ceasefire.

items.title: Sebi amends FPIs rule; directs registration fee payment in Indian rupee
items.summary: Sebi has announced a modification to its foreign investor registration fees, shifting to a rupee-based model effective in six months. This adjustment stipulates that Category-I FPIs and FVCIs will be required to pay Rs 2.3 lakh. Additionally, the common application will now demand the date of birth from applicants, and depository participants must ensure that all fees are forwarded to Sebi within a five-day period.

items.title: 10-year Treasury yield jumps to 4.57% as jumping oil prices reignite inflation fears
items.summary: Treasury yields surged after President Donald Trump told the NATO summit that the ceasefire with Iran is over.

items.title: GJEPC prepares ground for FTA with UK from July 15
items.summary: GJEPC estimates that India’s gem and jewellery exports to the UK can more than triple from $2.5 billion to $7 billion over the coming years on the back of bilateral trade

items.title: Amazon Seller Services leases over 4.17 lakh sq ft warehouse in Bhiwandi near Mumbai
items.summary: Amazon India has taken a warehousing facility in Bhiwandi through a long-term lease, set for nine years beginning June 2026. The financial commitment involves a monthly rent exceeding Rs 1.27 crore. This strategic move highlights Amazon's commitment to enhancing its logistics infrastructure and fortifying its supply chain.

items.title: Biometric UPI transactions top 600 million in June, says NPCI
items.summary: Biometric UPI transactions surpassed 600 million in June, indicating strong user preference. This secure authentication method reduces reliance on UPI PINs for faster payments. The feature supports various transactions, including RuPay Credit Card on UPI payments. Banks and UPI apps are increasingly adopting this convenient and efficient payment solution. Wider adoption is expected to further solidify UPI's position as India's preferred digital platform.

items.title: Virgin Media fined after hanging up on customers trying to cancel contracts
items.summary: Millions of phone calls from customers were "likely mishandled" over nearly a three-year period, the regulator says.

items.title: EV push threatens CNG and PNG market as city gas firms brace for volume and price pressure: Report
items.summary: Electric vehicles are gaining ground fast in key city gas areas and the surge is starting to eat into CNG market share and could weigh on long-term PNG demand too, according to a report by brokerage firm Dolat Capital. This comes in contrast to City Gas Distributors cutting prices to defend market share against propane.

items.title: Why AI literacy is the most important skill for students entering the job market
items.summary: Employers now expect AI-ready graduates. This article explores why AI literacy is essential for students and how ET Masterclass's new program bridges the classroom-to-career skills gap.

items.title: Crude oil price surges more than 6% after Trump says ceasefire with Iran is 'over'
items.summary: Brent crude price jumps 6.3% to $78.80 a barrel

items.title: Microsoft layoff: Employees offered up to 39 weeks' pay, stock vesting; here's who gets what
items.summary: Microsoft announced layoffs affecting 4,800 employees, offering severance packages up to 39 weeks' pay. Compensation varies by employee level, with options for continued stock vesting and health insurance. The cuts aim to streamline costs amid significant AI investments.

items.title: Inside the world’s biggest battery maker
items.summary: What does CATL’s extraordinary rise mean for the world?

items.title: India silver import curbs create shortages, push premiums to six-month high
items.summary: India's silver import restrictions have caused significant shortages in the domestic market. Premiums have surged to their highest levels in six months due to these curbs. May imports saw a drastic fall from the previous year's figures. These measures aim to ease pressure on foreign exchange reserves and the rupee. Domestic supplies are now largely dependent on local production.

items.title: DPR underway for Ghaziabad-Jewar RRTS corridor to cut travel time to Noida airport
items.summary: The Ghaziabad-Jewar Namo Bharat corridor has advanced to the Detailed Project Report stage. This new rapid transit line will connect Ghaziabad to Noida International Airport. The corridor is planned to feature twelve stations and two depots along its route. It aims to significantly reduce travel times between key NCR cities and the airport.

items.title: Trump pours cold water on NATO allies' united front
items.summary: Trump criticized NATO allies over defense spending, attacked Spain and renewed his Greenland push during the alliance’s summit in Turkey.

items.title: Top Gainers & Losers on 8 July: Aegis Logistics, HPCL, Bank of India, IndiGo, Coforge, MRF among top losers
items.summary: The Indian stock market faced heavy selling on Wednesday, with benchmark indices falling over 2% amid renewed U.S.-Iran tensions. The Nifty 50 dropped to 23,882, marking its biggest decline since March, and nearly all sectoral indices ended in the red, particularly the Nifty PSU Bank index.

items.title: Trump says he doesn't want anything to do with Spain: 'Cut off all trade'
items.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump slammed Spain for not contributing enough to NATO, as he attended the defense alliance summit in Turkey.

items.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Highlights: Sensex tumbles 1,677 points, Nifty slips below 23,900 after Trump’s Iran comments
items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Share Prices Highlights: Indian benchmark indices slumped over 2 per cent in late trade after US President Donald Trump declared Iran peace negotiations were effectively over, reigniting fears of escalating West Asia tensions, surging oil prices and heightened market volatility, triggering broad-based selling across sectors.

items.title: Can Virat Kohli crack India’s celebrity-brand jinx?
items.summary: Virat Kohli relaunched his lifestyle brand One8 with Agilitas Sports. This venture aims to overcome past challenges faced by celebrity-backed brands. Agilitas brings operational expertise and manufacturing capabilities to the brand. The company is focusing on rapid product development and efficient distribution. Success will depend on consumer repeat purchases within the first year.

items.title: Knack Packaging shares end with over 7% listing gains
items.summary: According to an analyst the company is supported by healthy revenue growth, improving profitability and high return on equity; customer concentration risk and execution of the new manufacturing facility are the factors to watch

items.title: Crude oil prices jump 6%, hit two-week high after Trump says Iran peace deal is ‘over’. What are experts saying?
items.summary: Crude oil prices rose sharply on Wednesday to a two-week high after Donald Trump declared the Iran peace agreement "over", reviving fears of fresh supply disruptions from the Middle East. Analysts say concerns over the Strait of Hormuz, tighter sanctions and depleted inventories could keep oil prices elevated in the near term.

items.title: Gold price falls ₹2,308/10g in futures trade as higher crude, strong US dollar dampen bullion demand
items.summary: In the international markets, Comex gold futures for August delivery fell $94.69, or 2.28%, to $4,062.71 per ounce in New York

items.title: Pidilite sees stable demand in FY27, raises concerns over higher input costs
items.summary: Pidilite Industries expects stable domestic demand in FY27, supported by government policies and infrastructure investment. However, rising input costs and supply chain disruptions pose significant concerns for the company. The West Asia conflict has driven up prices of key raw materials like Vinyl Acetate Monomer. Despite these challenges, the company achieved improved volume growth in FY25-26.

items.title: Torrent Pharma among 6 stocks to hit 52-week highs & rally up to 25% in a month
items.summary: Despite a sharp sell-off in the broader market, six BSE 200 stocks touched fresh 52-week highs, highlighting resilient investor confidence. The outperformers from retail, pharmaceuticals and energy sectors continued to show strong momentum, signalling sustained buying interest despite market volatility.

items.title: India eats 4 lakh rotis on trains as Swiggy's rail food orders triple in Q1FY27
items.summary: Swiggy's rail food delivery saw threefold growth in the last quarter. Indian train travelers ordered over four lakh rotis during the summer months. Multi-station orders surged more than 300 percent year-on-year, indicating increased passenger engagement. This service is also attracting first-time users in smaller towns and cities. Popular food items included roti, masala dosa, and various summer beverages.

items.title: Cult. Fit IPO: Actor Hrithik Roshan set to sell 6.33 lakh shares in Rs 3,500-4,000-crore offer
items.summary: Actor Hrithik Roshan plans to sell shares in Cult. Fit's upcoming public offering. The company aims to raise Rs 3,500-4,000 crore through its initial public offering. This IPO includes a fresh issue and an offer for sale by existing investors.

items.title: Rupee slumps to one-month low as oil soars after Trump says Iran deal is 'over'
items.summary: The Indian rupee fell to its weakest level in nearly a month on Wednesday. This decline occurred after U.S. President Donald Trump stated the interim accord with Iran was over. Oil prices jumped significantly, renewing fears of Middle East supply disruptions. Indian stocks also dropped sharply, their steepest fall in over three months. Elevated oil prices are expected to pressure the rupee and slow economic growth.

items.title: Apple commits $30 billion to Broadcom for U.S. chipmaking push
items.summary: Apple is expanding its Broadcom partnership in a $30 billion-plus chipmaking agreement, its largest American manufacturing commitment to date.

items.title: NCERT revises Class 8 textbook: Partition narrative tweaked, Savarkar added, Hitler references removed
items.summary: NCERT has revised its Class 8 social science textbook with several significant changes. The Partition chapter now presents a debated acceptance of the division, and VD Savarkar's Swaraj demand is included. References to Adolf Hitler and Nazi ideology have been removed from a freedom struggle chapter. The judiciary chapter was rewritten after Supreme Court intervention and now includes new legal content.

items.title: US futures tumble as Trump says Iran deal is 'over'; oil climbs
items.summary: On Wednesday morning, U.S. stock futures took a significant hit as President Donald Trump declared the memorandum of understanding with Iran null and void. This announcement sparked a notable rise in oil prices, leading to a boost in energy stocks during premarket trading. Meanwhile, investors await the release of the minutes from the U.S. Federal Reserve meeting for further insights.

items.title: Kusumgar IPO Day 1: Opens today at ₹398-419 price band
items.summary: Mops up ₹194 crore from anchor investors

items.title: Trump says Iran ceasefire is 'over' after latest round of strikes
items.summary: The president was asked about the ceasefire after an escalation of fighting in the Middle East.

items.title: Women investors account for over 13% of crypto futures traders; XRP and Bitcoin top their portfolios
items.summary: Crypto futures trading in India saw significant growth from smaller cities. Women traders showed lower leverage and preferred specific digital assets. Bitcoin and Ethereum comprised a smaller portion of futures volume. Tamil Nadu led regional participation in crypto futures trading. Traders demonstrated tactical approaches and risk awareness in leveraged products.

items.title: India's energy storage requirement set to jump from 1 GWh to 888 GWh by 2035-36: Report
items.summary: India’s vision for 2035-36 includes a staggering demand of 888 GWh for energy storage, significantly up from today’s capacity. This development is critical not only for maintaining grid reliability but also for the seamless integration of renewable energies. In a mere six months, installed energy storage capabilities grew elevenfold, and the momentum continues with more tenders anticipated.

items.title: Opportunity worth $35 billion for medtech sector in India by 2030: Bain & Co
items.summary: India's medtech sector presents a USD 35 billion opportunity by 2030. Exports are projected to reach USD 8 billion, growing over twenty percent annually. The nation is becoming a prominent access-led innovator in the Asia-Pacific region. This growth is driven by increasing healthcare demand and a developing innovation ecosystem. India's medical device exports reached USD 4 billion in FY25.

items.title: The Newton’s Cradle of credit today
items.summary: ‘Technicals have not merely supported fundamental pricing this cycle — they have displaced it’

items.title: Trump opposes Carroll getting $5 million damages verdict, cites last-ditch Supreme Court bid
items.summary: Trump has been found liable in two trials for defaming E. Jean Carroll when he denied her claim he sexually abused her in a New York department store.

items.title: Flipkart extends zero-commission policy to all fashion products, benefiting 90,000 sellers
items.summary: Flipkart has removed its Rs 1,000 price cap on fashion items. This change now covers all products in the fashion category without any commission. Approximately 90,000 active sellers in fashion will now retain their full margins. This seller-first initiative is a long-term investment for innovation and brand building.

items.title: Leh airport's new terminal 83% complete, to triple flight handling capacity: Civil Aviation Minister
items.summary: Leh airport's new integrated terminal building is eighty-three percent physically complete. This new facility will significantly enhance air connectivity to the strategically important Himalayan region. Once operational, the terminal will handle up to fifty-four flights daily, tripling current capacity. The expansion aims to strengthen connectivity and support the region's growing travel needs.

items.title: Insects in your fuel tank? ISMA responds to claims of E20-led car damage & sugarcane mixing claims
items.summary: E20 petrol is safe and scientifically validated, ISMA said, dismissing claims that it damages vehicles, attracts insects, voids insurance or uses sugarcane juice directly as fuel. Backing the government's stance, the industry body said no engine failures have been linked to E20 since its rollout and urged reliance on verified facts.

items.title: Rajasthan refinery has used as much steel as can build 40 Eiffel Towers and 5 Burj Khalifas: Modi
Family behind top US egg producer reaped $320mn after price surge
items.summary: Descendants of Cal-Maine founder sold controlling stake near a record high after what authorities said was an effort to rig the market

items.title: NCERT says all but two new textbooks for Classes 1-9 available; remaining titles by August
items.summary: NCERT has released most new textbooks for Classes 1 to 9. Remaining Class 9 social science and mathematics books will be available by August. New textbooks for Classes 10 and 11 are deferred to the 2027-28 academic session. Existing textbooks for these classes will continue to be used. These revised books align with the NCF-SE 2023 framework.

items.title: Fortis Healthcare board okays extending MD & CEO Ashutosh Raghuvanshi's tenure by 2 years
items.summary: Fortis Healthcare's board has approved Ashutosh Raghuvanshi's reappointment as Managing Director. His new term will commence on March 19, 2027, for a period of two years. This reappointment follows the conclusion of his current tenure on March 18, 2027. The board cited his expertise and role in the company's growth for this decision.

items.title: MobiKwik CFO Taku calls for MDR on UPI transactions
items.summary: Upasana Taku said that while the industry supports the government's financial inclusion goals, the absence of merchant discount rate means payment firms and banks continue to bear the cost of processing transactions without any revenue, even as large listed firms benefit from the zero-fee regime.

items.title: Tata Motors charts $100 billion automotive ambition, commits Rs 40,000 crore to India business
items.summary: Tata Motors aims for a USD 100 billion automotive business by FY31. The company plans substantial capital expenditure for domestic operations and JLR. Passenger vehicles will focus on aspirational products and market share growth. Jaguar Land Rover will contribute significantly to the projected sales figures. Digital technologies and AI are key investments for future operations.

items.title: ICICI Prudential Life Insurance reports 14.6% APE growth in June quarter, stocks in red
items.summary: Retail Weighted Received Premium (RWRP) for Q1-FY2027 grew 13.4% to ₹15.38 billion, with June contributing ₹6.23 billion, a 12.7% year-on-year increase

items.title: Trump doubles down on push for control over Greenland as Denmark vows to defend it
items.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump on Wednesday said the U.S. needs to control Greenland “for the protection of the world."

items.title: GMR Airports unveils multi-phase expansion roadmap for Nagpur Airport
items.summary: GMR Airports Limited announced a significant expansion plan for Nagpur Airport. This multi-phase roadmap aims to enhance passenger experience and operational capacity. The project includes an initial investment of approximately Rs 300 crore for immediate upgrades. Future plans envision increasing passenger handling capacity to thirty million annually. The airport will also develop into a major cargo and logistics hub.

items.title: Gold falls, crude oil rises after Trump says Iran peace deal ‘over’
items.summary: Trump says interim accord with Iran to end war is ‘over’

items.title: The places where it's cheaper to holiday this summer
items.summary: Family deals to many non-European destinations are cheaper this summer than last, as travel nerves have slowed bookings.

items.title: Private life insurers outpace LIC as industry new business premium rises 16.6% in Q1
items.summary: Life insurers saw a 16.6% rise in new business premium during the first quarter. Private sector players grew faster than LIC, driven by regular premium products. Total new business premium collected reached Rs 1.09 lakh crore in the period. Insurers are preparing for upcoming regulatory changes and improving demand. Private companies gained market share while LIC retained its dominant position.

items.title: UniCredit secures 48% stake in Commerzbank
items.summary: Italian bank closing in on majority control of German lender after end of tender period

items.title: From mouthwash to hair dye: How weight-loss jabs are changing shopping habits
items.summary: The BBC looks at how spending habits have shifted as users report feeling less hungry.

items.title: Oberoi Realty’s NCR debut spurs growth visibility with solid bookings
items.summary: Oberoi Realty's debut NCR launch, Three Sixty North, booked ₹8,109 crore—more than the company's entire FY26 sales. Brokerages have raised price targets and growth estimates, though a heavy MMR-focused pipeline and demand risks remain.

items.title: BPCL, HPCL to IOC: OMC stocks fall up to 5.5% on sharp rebound in crude oil prices
items.summary: State-owned oil marketing companies HPCL, BPCL, and IOC saw their shares drop as crude oil prices surged due to Middle East tensions. Investors booked profits after price declines, with HPCL down 5.4%, BPCL 4.7%, and IOC 3.5%. Existing conditions may pressure OMCs' profit margins.

items.title: The new rules of brand authority: Why credibility matters more than ever
items.summary: Building a recognised brand takes more than reach. As organisations compete for the attention of business leaders, trusted platforms and meaningful engagement are becoming central to long-term brand authority.

items.title: Sinopec unit signs engineering deal for Central Asia's first large-scale integrated biojet fuel complex
items.summary: Sinopec Engineering will design Central Asia's first large-scale sustainable aviation fuel complex. This project in Uzbekistan will produce biomass-based SAF and e-SAF. The complex will utilize sorghum, rice husks, and straw as its primary feedstock. Solar power will serve as the main electricity source for the plant. This initiative supports Sinopec's low-carbon energy goals and Uzbekistan's aviation hub ambitions.

items.title: Global Market: European shares decline as Middle East tensions weigh; airlines, autos lead losses
items.summary: European shares declined on Wednesday as renewed Middle East tensions and higher oil prices weighed on investor sentiment, while technology stocks traded mixed amid concerns over the sustainability of the AI-driven rally. Airline, banking and auto stocks led the losses, although select technology names and Swedish broadband provider Bahnhof outperformed.

items.title: PhonePe appoints Srijon Biswas as chief technology officer
items.summary: Biswas will lead the fintech firm's engineering organisation as PhonePe doubles down on technology and platform investments.

items.title: Oil prices jump to more than $78 a barrel as Trump says ceasefire with Iran is 'over'
items.summary: Oil prices rose nearly 6% after US President Trump declared the interim agreement with Iran is over. Details here.

items.title: SAIL, Indonesian firm to explore JV for stainless steel manufacturing
items.summary: SAIL and Indonesia's PT Krakatau Steel will explore a joint venture for stainless steel slab production. This collaboration aims to meet India's growing demand for the commodity. Indonesia offers rich nickel reserves, a key raw material for stainless steel. SAIL brings extensive experience in steelmaking and operating large plants. This partnership strengthens industrial ties and manufacturing value chains between the two nations.

items.title: SambaNova hits $11 billion valuation as investors back Nvidia chip challengers
items.summary: SambaNova is valued at $11 billion after fresh financing led by General Atlantic, as AI chip startups look to challenge Nvidia.

items.title: Defence sector Q1 results preview: Muted earnings likely on execution delays; BEL, Bharat Dynamics, GRSE among top picks
items.summary: Defence sector Q1 results preview: While reported earnings of defence sector may remain subdued, underlying execution momentum is gradually building, with clearer visibility on recovery expected as conversion accelerates through the year, particularly into H2FY27, analysts said.

items.title: Cultural sensitivity, communication key to strengthening India's medical tourism appeal: IIM experts
items.summary: Experts highlight cultural sensitivity and communication's importance for international medical tourists. Effective expectation management and communication enhance patient experiences in Indian hospitals. India is a leading medical tourism destination due to advanced healthcare and affordable costs. Hospitals should invest in intercultural communication training for professionals. Bridging cultural gaps improves patient satisfaction and India's market competitiveness.

items.title: Prolonged US-Iran tensions may push India's crude basket above $75/bbl, raise inflation risks: Experts
items.summary: Market experts warn a prolonged US-Iran standoff could increase India's crude oil import costs. Diversified sourcing is expected to cushion supply disruptions, reducing vulnerability to shocks. However, prolonged conflict may push India's crude basket beyond $75 per barrel. Higher crude prices could create near-term macroeconomic pressures and inflation risks.

items.title: South Indian Bank says RBI approves Mahesh Muralidhar Pai's appointment as MD, CEO
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has approved Mahesh Muralidhar Pai's appointment as Managing Director and CEO. This appointment is for a three-year term, commencing on October 1, 2026. The bank's board will consider this approval at their upcoming July 16 meeting. Shareholders will then provide their necessary approval for the appointment. South Indian Bank shares experienced a significant decline following this announcement.

items.title: FSSAI notices to Lotte India, Ferns N Petals, Kubera Foods for misleading claims
items.summary: Food regulator FSSAI has issued notices to three companies for labelling violations. Lotte India faced action for non-compliant labels and misleading vegetarian claims. Kubera Foods received a notice for false claims on its cream bun product. Ferns N Petals was also cited for misleading claims on its chocolate. These actions aim to protect consumer interests and ensure fair practices.

items.title: Andhra IT Minister Lokesh invites South Korean BNK Venture Capital to mentor SMEs, startups
items.summary: Andhra Pradesh's IT Minister Nara Lokesh has opened doors to South Korean BNK Venture Capital, seeking mentorship for local SMEs. items.summary: A multi-year capital expansion plan will follow the $15-billion investment in Andhra Pradesh announced last October, Thomas Kurian, chief executive of Google Cloud, told Mint.

items.title: After nearly a decade of decline, DXC lays out a turnaround plan
items.summary: DXC’s management on Tuesday outlined a turnaround strategy at a media briefing held alongside the inauguration of a new office in Bengaluru.

items.title: Victims of 23andMe data breach to get $47m payout, judge rules
items.summary: 23andMe compiles genetic profiles of people through DNA testing kits, but it was heavily criticised after a 2023 hack.

items.title: Netflix, Disney and YouTube interested in FIFA World Cup U.S. rights; package could reach $2 billion
items.summary: FIFA has alerted media companies that English- and Spanish-language U.S. rights are likely to be sold together for 2030 and 2034, likely driving up the price.

items.title: U.S. revokes Iran oil sales authorization after tanker attacks
items.summary: Treasury had waived sanctions on Iranian oil through Aug. 21 after Washington and Tehran inked an interim deal to reopen Hormuz last month.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz threat level raised to 'severe' after Iran attacks tankers using U.S. Navy route
items.summary: Iran's military has warned it will target ships that do not use a northern route approved by Tehran.

items.title: Software stocks got crushed. Did they have it coming?
items.summary: The market may be overreacting to AI risks faced by software companies

items.title: Sugar prices up 6-7% in June amid below-normal rainfall
items.summary: Sugar prices have increased six to seven percent over June. This rise occurred as below-normal rainfall raised concerns about crop output. Demand also climbed with the end of the Hindu month of Adhik Maas. Ex-mill sugar prices in Maharashtra have increased to about forty-one point five rupees per kilogram. Recent rainfall is expected to provide relief to the standing sugarcane crop.

items.title: Kharif sowing slow, 15 more districts face rain deficiency
items.summary: The government is monitoring 262 districts for rainfall deficiency and El Nino impacts. Fifteen additional districts have been identified as facing rainfall deficits. Sowing remains slower than usual due to uneven rainfall across the country. However, rains are forecast for Gujarat, Haryana, Punjab, and Rajasthan soon. Contingency plans are being implemented to minimize potential impacts on agriculture.

items.title: India asks US to reconsider proposed 12.5% additional tariff
items.summary: India has asked the US to reconsider proposed additional tariffs on its exports. New Delhi argues the proposed tariffs lack legal basis and sufficient evidence. The US Trade Representative is investigating dozens of economies for forced labor import policies. India believes the US has not conducted economy-specific analyses of these practices. Dialogue is preferred to address any specific concerns raised by the US.

items.title: Economic Advisory Council to the Prime Minister calls for rapid scale-up of grid-scale battery storage
items.summary: India's electricity challenge has shifted from generation to managing availability and flexibility. Rising solar power causes grid stress and price volatility between day and night. The government recognises energy storage as crucial for the power system. Expanding solar capacity needs corresponding investments in storage solutions. This ensures grid stability and meets evening peak demand effectively.

items.title: Information & Computer services to make up 1/5th of India's proposed planned index
items.summary: India will soon release a new monthly Index of Services Production. Information and computer services will lead this important economic indicator. Retail trade and administrative support will follow as significant components. This index will cover the formal services sector and use GST data. The first trial release is scheduled for July 2026.

items.title: Ministries asked to fill top vacancies at CPSEs in 3 months
items.summary: The government has issued a directive mandating that ministries address top management vacancies within a strict three-month timeframe. Delays in filling these positions have been hindering important strategic decisions in various state-owned enterprises.

items.title: RBI mulls guidelines on property title searches
items.summary: The guidelines could prescribe a minimum search period, key documents and records to be verified, as well as precautions to be followed while preparing title search or legal background check reports, said people familiar with the development.

items.title: Air-cooled condenser makers urge review of Chinese firms' entry into thermal power contracts
items.summary: ACC systems are used in dry-cooling systems at thermal power plants and are typically sub-contracted as part of larger engineering, procurement and construction contracts.

items.title: Meta enters AI image model race in bid to court advertisers and subscribers
items.summary: Meta has announced Muse Image, its first AI model for image creation, as it seeks to attract creators and advertisers to its offerings.

items.title: Inspira Global completes ₹2,235-crore Restaurant Brands Asia acquisition
items.summary: The deal gives Inspira control of Burger King India and Indonesia operations of Burger King and Popeyes, as consolidation gathers pace in the QSR industry.

items.title: Meta says child abuse ‘horrific crime’, details fight against CSAM ads on Instagram after govt notice
items.summary: Meta breaks its silence on Instagram CSAM ad row, detailing AI tools and account removals after MeitY's notice and a BBC investigation.

items.title: Trump renews Greenland threats at NATO summit, says U.S. could remove troops from Europe
items.summary: NATO fell into a crisis earlier this year as Trump demanded that the U.S. must take control of Greenland on national security grounds.

items.title: Ukraine has already won the war against Russia, Finnish president tells CNBC
items.summary: Ukraine needs NATO, but NATO needs Ukraine just as much, the Finnish president told CNBC on Tuesday.

items.title: Reliance's Strand Life Sciences secures Indian patent for blood-based early cancer detection
items.summary: The patented platform combines genome sequencing, methylation analysis and machine learning to detect cancer and identify its tissue of origin from a single blood sample.

items.title: India's auto parts exports to US remain strong despite tariff tensions
items.summary: The United States remains India's largest auto parts export market, accounting for twenty-six percent of shipments. India's auto component exports grew five percent to twenty-four billion dollars last fiscal year. Investigations into unfair practices are underway by the United States Trade Representative's office. Indian firms are receiving requests for quotations from US companies seeking to reduce China dependence.

items.title: Top banking watchdogs issue stark warning over AI-driven cyber attacks
items.summary: IT weaknesses could be exploited by frontier models in a ‘matter of minutes’, say ECB and ESRB

items.title: Govt extends additional investment choices under NPS to autonomous bodies
items.summary: The government has extended additional National Pension System investment choices. These options are now available to employees of central autonomous bodies. Aggressive Life Cycle Fund LC-75-High offers up to seventy-five percent equity exposure. Balanced Life Cycle Fund provides a fifty percent equity cap. This move enhances subscriber flexibility and pension planning options.

items.title: FDI inflows to India increased by 44% to $39 billion in 2025: UN
items.summary: India's foreign direct investment increased by forty-four percent to USD thirty-nine billion in 2025. Global foreign direct investment showed resilience, rising six percent to USD one point six trillion. Developed economies saw inflows increase eleven percent, while developing economies rose two percent. Announced greenfield investment in India declined, indicating a cautious investment cycle. However, services investment remained resilient, with ICT becoming the largest sector.

items.title: USTR's Sec 301 probe on forced labour concerns: India urges reconsideration of proposed 12.5 pc tariffs
items.summary: India has asked the US to reconsider proposed tariffs on its goods. The US Trade Representative is considering a 12.5 percent tariff on Indian imports. India disputes the US findings regarding forced labor and market distortion. The country is willing to engage in dialogue to address US concerns. Final decisions on these proposed tariffs are pending further review.

items.title: One million more UK homeowners set to face higher mortgages
items.summary: Homeowners face paying £45 a month more on average when they move onto a new deal over the next two years.

items.title: Bank of England plans to ease capital rule for UK lenders
items.summary: Officials say move intended to boost lending and support financial markets in a crisis

items.title: CCI okays upGrad-Unacademy merger, clears path for major edtech consolidation; details here
items.summary: The Competition Commission of India has approved upGrad's acquisition of Unacademy's parent company, Sorting Hat Technologies.

items.title: Online gamblers betting more than £1,000 to face new checks
items.summary: Punters who bet more than £1,000 online in a 24-hour window will have to undergo an assessment, the regulator says.

items.title: Trump ally Nigel Farage quits UK parliament amid finance scandal to fight special election
items.summary: Reform U.K. leader Nigel Farage has quit as MP for Clacton, forcing a by-election that he has said he will fight.

items.title: Cult.fit bets beyond gyms while remaining hooked on India's top four cities
items.summary: Cult.fit is expanding beyond gym memberships through products, corporate wellness, advertising and franchises, even as its business remains heavily reliant on fitness centres in India's four largest cities.

items.title: India climbs global manufacturing rankings amid China+1 shift
items.summary: India's manufacturing growth has surpassed the global average, positioning it as an emerging leader. This rise is fueled by domestic demand and government initiatives like PLI schemes. Global companies are seeking diversification and resilience in their supply chains. Investor confidence is growing due to sustained reforms and strategic initiatives. India's economy continues its robust expansion, reinforcing its manufacturing strength.

items.title: Ukrainian drones hit Russia’s largest oil refinery as Zelenskyy says Siberia now ‘within reach’
items.summary: The attack underscores Kyiv's enhanced long-range drone capabilities and comes on the eve of a crucial NATO summit.

items.title: Expert panel: Release services production index within 60 days
items.summary: An expert panel recommended monthly compilation of sectoral indices and releasing the ISP within sixty days. This new index will provide a comprehensive monthly measure of India's formal sector. The proposed ISP framework aims to strengthen statistical systems for the services economy. It will complement the Index of Industrial Production for better economic monitoring. The trial ISP series is scheduled for release on July fourteenth, twenty twenty-six.

items.title: London IPO candidate CFC hires ex-Direct Line chief as new boss
items.summary: Private equity-backed business is exploring strategic options including a potential listing on the LSE

items.title: Why oil investors fear the next toll fight could be the Strait of Malacca
items.summary: The prospect of fees to transit the Strait of Hormuz has sparked alarm, not least by investors who fear it could be replicated in other maritime corridors.

items.title: Godrej Pet Care to build broader pet products portfolio after Ninja debut
items.summary: Godrej Pet Care plans to expand Ninja beyond dry dog food into wet food, treats and cat food as it targets India's fast-growing pet nutrition market dominated by Mars and Drools.

items.title: Dissecting the death of overnight drift
Beyond generics: Glenmark resets agenda with a bold 10-year pivot to global drug innovation, says chairman Saldanha
items.summary: For chairman Glenn Saldanha, the way forward is a mix of licensing deals, partnerships and Glenmark’s own commercialization in emerging markets.

items.title: E-bike injury payouts top £110m and push up insurance premiums
items.summary: It is only seven years since the first claim over an injury from a micromobility vehicle was made.

items.title: ECLGS 5.0 crosses 4.11 lakh guarantees, extends Rs 1.55 lakh crore support to firms hit by West Asia crisis
items.summary: The Emergency Credit Line Guarantee Scheme 5.0 has issued over 4.11 lakh guarantees. These guarantees amount to more than Rs 1.55 lakh crore for affected businesses. Micro, small, and medium enterprises received the vast majority of these guarantees. The scheme aims to provide additional liquidity support to eligible borrowers. This initiative helps businesses manage disruptions from the West Asia crisis.

items.title: US Forced Labour Hearings: Trump's next tariff threat looms over 60 countries, including India
items.summary: The United States is considering new tariffs on sixty nations, including India, due to forced labor concerns. Hearings will begin to discuss potential import duties on goods suspected of forced labor origins. India has rejected these allegations and is presenting its case to US trade officials. This move could impact ongoing trade talks and existing tariff structures. The administration plans to reinstate tariffs similar to those seen previously.

items.title: Hotel chains loosen purse strings for marquee asset makeovers
items.summary: Leading Indian hotel chains aim to enhance their pricing power and profit margins by refurbishing their existing marquee properties.

items.summary: Authorities raise quota for offshore Connect scheme in bid to boost international use of renminbi

items.title: Report proposes 'India-Plus Contribution Scorecard' to boost FDI
items.summary: A new report suggests an 'India-Plus Contribution Scorecard' for foreign investment. This framework would rate investors on economic contributions like exports and technology. Companies scoring above seventy would receive a ninety-day single-window clearance. National security screening will remain a stringent requirement for all foreign investments. This approach aims to attract rooted, contribution-generating investment for India's economy.

items.title: FMCG companies hopeful of healthy Q1 growth despite inflationary headwinds
items.summary: Calibrated price hikes have helped Indian FMCG companies navigate cost volatility without adversely affecting sales volumes

items.title: Singapore's Temasek to lead space tech startup Pixxel's $100 million round
items.summary: Temasek is leading a $100 million round in Indian space-tech startup Pixxel at a $350-400 million valuation, joining GIC in backing the satellite imaging firm. The deal adds to a broader rush of capital into space startups, including recent large rounds for Skyroot, Digantara, and Agnikul Cosmos.

items.title: Samsung profits jump 1,800% as AI chip sales soar
items.summary: The firm's shares fell sharply on Tuesday as some investors had expected an even stronger performance.

items.title: Gush of Gulf sovereign wealth enters India in 2026
items.summary: Gulf sovereign funds invested $1.7 billion in India during the first half of 2026. This investment defied geopolitical tensions and fears of reduced overseas deal appetite. The region's share in India's sovereign wealth fund investments nearly doubled since 2024. Funds deployed nearly three times more capital compared to the second half of 2025. These investors prioritize long-term structural themes like infrastructure and technology.

items.title: IT giants delay fresher onboarding as business uncertainty clouds campus offers
items.summary: India's largest IT recruiters are delaying onboarding and, in some cases, withdrawing offers made to engineering graduates, reflecting a more cautious approach to hiring amid uneven client demand and broader macroeconomic uncertainty.

items.title: Kotak-backed Biorad Medisys in talks to raise ₹400 crore ahead of a potential IPO
items.summary: Medical devices maker Biorad Medisys is raising about ₹400 crore in a pre-IPO round at a ₹10,000 crore valuation as it prepares for a potential ₹1,500 crore public listing.

items.title: Fall in crude prices not enough to lift Rupee: Mint poll
items.summary: Economists predict the Indian rupee will remain stable at 94-96 against the US dollar despite falling crude oil prices. Unlikely to appreciate significantly, the rupee is expected to gradually weaken to around 96.5 by March 2027, as central bank policies absorb dollar inflows.

items.title: In a flurry of deals, Adani mobilizes $10 bn in a week for capex boost
items.summary: The fund mobilization spans equity raises, stake sales and long-term commitments from strategic partners, including global investors and sovereign-backed entities. The flurry of deals marks one of the largest capital-raising exercises by an Indian conglomerate in recent years.

items.title: Skyroot Aerospace: building the Uber for space, one launch at a time
items.summary: Skyroot is preparing for Vikram 1, a mission that will make it the first private Indian company to launch a commercial satellite. Co-founded by former Isro scientists Pawan Kumar Chandana and Naga Bharath Daka, the startup aims to capture a significant portion of the global launch market.

items.title: Beyond the rain hit, El Nino set to sap India's wind power output
items.summary: The El Nino weather pattern may do more than disrupt India's monsoon this year. It could also weaken wind speeds during the peak generation season, hurting India’s wind power output at a time of record electricity demand.

items.title: Robots available for rent: But what can they do?
items.summary: Robotics tech is changing fast, so for many it makes sense to rent a robot.

items.title: Amazon bars breastfeeding boss from business course
items.summary: Rachael Bews says she told on the way her child would not be allowed on site. Amazon has apologised for not communicating its policy clearly.

items.title: 'I wear it on my middle finger': The rise of the defiant divorce ring
items.summary: Around the world women are marking their split with new - or repurposed engagement - rings.

items.title: Banks accused of pushing customers away from basic accounts
items.summary: Some of the UK's biggest banks have been failing their most vulnerable customers, according to the financial regulator.

items.title: Phone contract comparisons 'amounted to mis-selling' student loans, MPs say
items.summary: A new report says students were not well-enough informed that their loan terms could change retrospectively.

items.title: Can China repeat its EV success with robotaxis?
items.summary: China's self-driving car firms have been given a headstart by the country's EV supply chain as they expand globally.

items.title: Auto companies may soon get to trade BEE credits for easier fuel-efficiency compliance
items.summary: The power ministry has introduced a new credit trading system aimed at Corporate Average Fuel Efficiency standards. Under this initiative, manufacturers can trade excess compliance credits and purchase them from the Bureau of Energy Efficiency. The framework includes credit and debit accounting, allowing manufacturers who surpass targets to earn credits, while those who don't accumulate debits.

items.title: India, EU aim to complete FTA legal scrubbing in 15-20 days, says Piyush Goyal
items.summary: Goyal will visit Spain, Belgium and Finland on July 14-15 with a delegation of business leaders as part of preparations for implementing the trade pact. He is also scheduled to meet EU Trade Commissioner Maros Sefcovic in Brussels. "We have already started marketing Indian goods and services so that we are future-ready to get the best out of the FTAs that we have finalised," Goyal said at an event organised by the Council for Leather Exports.

items.title: Kharif sowing picks up, but still 21% below last year
items.summary: Kharif sowing lags significantly behind last year due to delayed and uneven monsoon rains. Paddy, pulses, and oilseeds acreage shows substantial year-on-year declines. This reduced planting raises concerns about potential crop output and food inflation. Sugarcane acreage has seen a slight increase, bucking the overall trend. Below-normal rainfall forecasts for July further exacerbate these agricultural challenges.

items.title: Microsoft Layoffs: Xbox CEO Asha Sharma acknowledges 'business is not healthy', flags weak margins amid 3200 job cuts
items.summary: Announcing the overhaul in a message to employees on Monday, Xbox Chief Executive Officer Asha Sharma acknowledged the challenges facing the division. She said the restructuring is aimed at simplifying Xbox's operations and redirecting resources towards larger projects.

items.title: ICICI Prudential board approves reclassifying Prudential as an investor
items.summary: ICICI Prudential Life Insurance's board approved seeking Irdai's nod to reclassify Prudential Corp. Holdings and rename the company to ICICI Life Insurance Ltd.

items.title: Microsoft cuts 4,800 jobs and shrinks Xbox in 'significant restructure'
items.summary: The sweeping layoffs equate to 2.1% of Microsoft's workforce, with 1,600 immediate job losses at Xbox.

items.title: Lockheed Martin snaps up Ultra Maritime in $3.45 billion naval defence push: All about the deal
items.summary: The deal comes at a time when defence companies are benefiting from a surge in military spending worldwide.

items.title: ITV hits such as I'm a Celebrity to stay free to watch after Sky takeover
items.summary: Sky boss Dana Strong's comments came as the channel announces it is buying ITV's media and entertainment divisions in a £1.6bn deal.

items.title: LIC chief sees protection plans driving growth
items.summary: In the CEO message, R. Doraiswamy notes a shift towards term- and protection-oriented solutions and flags the pressure market volatility has placed on the insurer's participating funds.

items.title: NCR market will be as big or bigger than Mumbai for Oberoi Realty: Vikas Oberoi
items.summary: The Mumbai-based luxury developer's maiden project in Gurugram drew a staggering ₹30,000 crore order book, setting the stage for it to challenge established regional players in the booming northern market.

items.title: Saudi Arabia cuts oil prices for Asia customers by $11 a barrel in biggest reduction in 26 years
items.summary: Saudi Arabia cut the price of its main crude grade for customers in Asia in August by the most in at least 26 years, as a surge of global supply heightens competition for buyers.

items.title: Prayagraj records over 20% unemployment rate among 46 surveyed cities with 10 lakh+ population: Report
items.summary: Prayagraj and Patna recorded over 20% unemployment rates among 46 major cities, while Ahmedabad and Kolkata had the lowest at 2.5%. The average unemployment rate stands at 6.8%.
